content.problemStatement:
There are 333 problems in a contest namely A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C respectively. Alice bets Bob that problem CCC is the hardest while Bob says that problem BBB will be the hardest.

You are given three integers SA,SB,SCS_A, S_B, S_CSA​,SB​,SC​ which denotes the number of successful submissions of the problems A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C respectively. It is guaranteed that each problem has a different number of submissions. Determine who wins the bet.



If Alice wins the bet (i.e. problem CCC is the hardest), then output AliceAliceAlice.




If Bob wins the bet (i.e. problem BBB is the hardest), then output BobBobBob.




If no one wins the bet (i.e. problem AAA is the hardest), then output DrawDrawDraw.



**Note**: The hardest problem is the problem with the least number of successful submissions.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers SA,SB,SCS_A, S_B, S_CSA​,SB​,SC​, denoting the number of successful submissions of problems A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output the winner of the bet or print Draw in case no one wins the bet.
constraints: SA, SB, SC are pairwise distinct for each test case
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers SA, SB, SC — the numbers of successful submissions for problems A, B and C respectively. All three numbers in a test case are distinct.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one of the strings: "Alice" if problem C has the smallest number of submissions, "Bob" if problem B has the smallest number, or "Draw" if problem A has the smallest.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: minimum is 1 (problem A) → Draw.\nTest case 2: minimum is 8 (problem B) → Bob.\nTest case 3: minimum is 9 (problem C) → Alice.

solution.approach: For each test case read the three submission counts. Determine the minimum of the three values. If the minimum corresponds to SA output "Draw", if it corresponds to SB output "Bob", otherwise output "Alice". Because the three numbers are distinct, the minimum is unique.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
